LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES IS GRANTED.GOOD CAUSE APPEARING AND CONSISTENT WITH THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT AND CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6, JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED AS SUCH: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. SHALL RECOVER $14,149.09 IN PRINCIPAL, AND $563.32 IN COSTS, FOR A TOTAL OF $14,712.41. NO PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED JUDGMENT RECEIVED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE AND FOLLOW THE COURT'S ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND PROPOSED JUDGMENT AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: M. MACADANGDANG; NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To May-05-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A.'s unopposed Motion for Entry of Judgment Pursuant to Stipulation of the Parties is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as such: Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A. shall recover $14,149.09 in principal, and $563.32 in costs, for a total of $14,712.41.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the CCP and California Rules of Court, ideally on the Judicial Council form.

No proposed order and proposed judgment received. Moving Party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and proposed judgment and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
